name: Cell Cycle State Diagram with Nested Composite States
input: Create a state diagram for the eukaryotic cell cycle. Top-level states: Interphase and M Phase (Mitosis). Interphase is a composite state containing: G1 (Gap 1 — cell growth), S (DNA Synthesis), G2 (Gap 2 — preparation for mitosis). Transition from G1 to S requires passing the G1/S checkpoint (Restriction Point). M Phase is a composite state containing: Prophase, Prometaphase, Metaphase, Anaphase, Telophase. Transition from G2 to M Phase requires passing the G2/M checkpoint. After Telophase -> Cytokinesis -> back to G1 (completing the cycle). Add a branch from G1: if cell receives quiescence signal -> G0 (quiescent state). From G0 -> can re-enter G1 with growth factor stimulation. Show the nested composite states clearly with the checkpoints as guard conditions on transitions.
qualityCriteria: Interphase as composite state containing G1, S, G2
M Phase as composite state containing Prophase through Telophase
G1/S checkpoint guard condition on G1 to S transition
G2/M checkpoint guard condition on transition to M Phase
G0 quiescent state with bidirectional transitions to G1
Cytokinesis state connecting back to G1 (cycle completion)
Valid Mermaid syntax that parses without errors
napkinBenchmark: stateDiagram-v2 with 2 composite states (Interphase, M Phase), nested sub-states, checkpoint guards, G0 branch, cyclic flow.
